q: Hermes is best described as which pricing model?
choices: BYOK — bring your own model API key
explain: Hermes is open-source and BYOK. You sign up with a model provider and pay per token. The harness itself is free; the model is not.

q: Which tool wins for 'coding all day, shipping production software'?
explain: Claude Code is the polished coding harness. It runs tests, integrates with your IDE, and verifies by execution — exactly what shipping software needs.

q: Per Ron, what is the right way to think about the four harnesses?
choices: Pick by workload — different tools win different jobs.
explain: There is no single winner. Different harnesses win different workloads. The decision is about your job, not the brand.
Mavis is a starter multi-agent platform, not a destination. Most users graduate to Hermes once they've learned the concepts.

q: Short answer: name one workload where Hermes clearly wins over Claude Code.
explain: Long-running automation (cron jobs, 24/7 scheduled tasks, multi-channel workflows like Discord/Telegram bots, Kanban, sub-agents). Claude Code is IDE-bound and was not designed for 24/7 background work.

q: What is the minimum time you should commit to a harness before switching?
choices: Switch immediately if anything goes wrong
explain: Switching cost is high. Muscle memory, Skills, MCP configs, Bundles — all tool-specific. Pick one and stick for at least 30 days.
